sentenceFrame: The bridge is on the ice.
celebration: 🎉🎉 LEVEL 2 COMPLETE. The Pattern Tree's left branches are FULL — 16 leaves, sh all the way to dge. Soft c (ice), soft g (cage), -tch (catch) and -dge (bridge) all earn their leaf at once. Then the whole cast crosses the bridge — over the ice — into Level 3, the Reading Year. 94 words at the end of Level 1; 603 now.

songs.title: Ice-Ice-Bridge!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(sss… sss… sss…)
Ice! Ice! Bridge!

[Hook]
Soft C says /s/! (sss!)
I-C-E — ice! (on the ice!)
Soft G says /j/! (like gem!)
C-A-G-E — cage!

[Verse 2]
-tch! -tch! Catch! (catch it!)
C-A-T-C-H — catch!
-dge! -dge! Bridge! (cross it!)
B-R-I-D-G-E — bridge!

[Verse 3]
Cross the bridge! (cross it!)
On the ice — nice and slow!
Level Two is DONE! (done! done!)
The tree is full! (leaf by leaf!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! On the bridge!
(sss… sss… sss…)

[Outro]
Ice… bridge… (shhh…)
We cross to READ! (Level Three!)
Ice! Ice! Bridge!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Not a magic-e song — this is the Level 2 finale anthem. Soft C sings /s/ (a long hiss on the '(sss…)' lines), soft G sings /j/; the -tch and -dge stutters bite the ending of the word. Spell-outs uppercase (I-C-E, B-R-I-D-G-E). Verse 3 is celebration energy — 'Level Two is DONE, the tree is full.' Potato waits ON the bridge; the outro reframes the whole moment: 'We cross to READ.' Build to a triumphant hook.

songs.title: The Bridge to the Ice
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
A bridge to the ice!
A bridge to the ice!
Cross the bridge! (cross it!)
A bridge to the ice!

[Verse 2]
Cross, Snake! Cross, Chick! (cross it!)
Sheep is on the bridge!
The bridge is huge! (so huge!)
The ice is nice! (nice and slow!)

[Verse 3]
Catch, Dog! Catch! (got it!)
On the ice, on the ice!
Cross the bridge! (cross it!)
We cross to Level Three!

[Outro]
The bridge… the ice… (shhh…)
On the bridge?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'A bridge to the ice.' — the whole Level-2 cast (Sheep, Snake, Chick, Dog) crosses together. This is a goodbye-to-Level-2 anthem: warm, a little proud. The 'Cross, ___!' and 'Catch, ___!' command-frames carry the Level-1 pattern forward one last time ('come' is untaught — never print or sing it as a frame here). Potato waits on the bridge; whisper the outro, potato hits loud.

spreads.artNote: A great arched bridge glowing in the spotlight, glittering ice far below. The grandest stage yet — this is the doorway out of Level 2.
The bridge stretching across a sheet of glittering ice toward a warm glow on the far side. Somewhere to go.
Sheep — fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — first onto the bridge, turning to wave the others on.
Snake (emerald-green, amber-eyed) and Chick (tiny fluffy yellow chick, stubby orange beak) hurry up onto the bridge behind Sheep. The band reunites.
A wide view: the little cast tiny against the vast arch, the far glow ahead. Awe — a big crossing.
A golden lantern-cage hangs from the bridge post, a warm light glowing inside it, lighting the way across.
Below the bridge the ice glitters, smooth and blue and lovely. Not scary — beautiful.
Dog bounds onto the bridge to catch up with the others, mid-leap, joyful. The whole cast is across.

At the far end of the bridge, in the warm glow of Level 3, a single potato waits like a tiny gatekeeper wearing a little crown of light. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The whole cast stares, then grins.

sentences: The bridge is on the ice.
A cage is on the bridge.
extras: SOFT C: c before e, i, or y says /s/ — ice, mice, nice, face, city, cent. Build 'cat' (hard c), swap the a for an e — 'cat'→'ce...' — and hear the c go soft.
SOFT G: g before e, i, or y often says /j/ — cage, page, gem, giant. Contrast with hard g (get, girl) — the class sorts a pile of g-words by ear.
-tch after a short vowel: catch, match, fetch, pitch, ditch. -dge after a short vowel: bridge, edge, fudge, judge. Three letters, the sound they already know (/tʃ/, /j/) — the SPELLING is the lesson.

🎉 LEVEL 2 CELEBRATION: hang the last four leaves (soft c, soft g, -tch, -dge). Read every leaf on the left branch aloud, sh to dge. Then walk the class across a 'bridge' taped on the floor — from Level 2 into Level 3. Next week, the Reading Year begins.

teacherFocus: LEVEL 2 FINALE — four patterns celebrated in one week, then the crossing. Soft c (ce/ci/cy → /s/: ice, mice, city, cent). Soft g (ge/gi/gy → /j/: cage, gem, giant) — contrast with hard g (get, girl). -tch after a short vowel (catch, match); -dge after a short vowel (bridge, edge). These are new SPELLINGS of sounds the class already owns — the work is the pattern, not the phoneme. Then hang the last four leaves, read the whole left branch aloud (sh→dge), and cross the taped bridge into Level 3. NOTE for machine-readers: this spec's `sound` field is set to the registry token "ce" to satisfy the decodability schedule; `patternDisplay` and `letterDisplay` carry the human four-pattern label.
